basics.name: Alexandre Vanryssel
basics.label: Tech Lead Freelance, Java & Web3
basics.summary: Experienced Tech Lead with over 15 years of experience in software development, specialized in Java and Spring Boot technologies. Expertise also covers blockchain (Ethereum, Tezos, Avalanche, Sui) and artificial intelligence. Technical leadership positions leading teams, designing robust and scalable architectures, and implementing good practices in quality, security, observability, and operations. DevOps and Cloud experience (Docker, Kubernetes, GCP, Azure). PSM I certified.

work.name: Adeo
work.summary: Tech lead in a team of 10 people working on several products around costing. Astek (until February 2025), then Freelance.
highlights: Architecture and technical design
Animation within the team of cross-cutting policies (quality, security, architecture, observability, operations)
Recruitment and onboarding
Development, CI/CD and Ops

Technical Environment: Java, Spring, Spring Boot, MongoDB, Mongo Atlas, Neo4J, Angular, Vue.js, Kafka, Docker, Kubernetes, GCP, Github, Github Actions, Github Copilot, Postman, Bruno, Gatling, Datadog

work.name: Marigold
work.summary: Engineering manager in the Tezos blockchain ecosystem. Manager of the team responsible for developing Ligo, Tezos' smart contract language.
highlights: Management of a team of 12 people distributed worldwide
Language evolution
Deployment of a library registry

work.name: Ineat Lab (Ineat Group), then Innovation Lab (Astek)
work.summary: Within Ineat's (then Astek's) Innovation cell, technological watch on blockchain and AI.
highlights: Blockchain: POCs and case studies, technological watch, internal training, response to calls for tenders, articles and meetups/conferences
AI: RAG study synthesizing processing errors of a business system
GraphRAG POC: knowledge graph to improve RAG search accuracy in the legal domain
Study of different models (Gemini, GPT, Llama, DeepSeek, Claude, Qwen)

Technical Environment: React, Javascript, Typescript, smart contracts, Ethereum (Solidity, Truffle, Remix, Web3.js), Tezos (Ligo, Taquito), Python, Jupyter, Ollama, OpenWebUI, Knowledge Graph, vectorization and embeddings, Atlas Vector Search, PGVector, Neo4J

work.name: Norauto (via Ineat Group)
work.summary: Tech lead in the Concept Store team. Implementation of proof of concepts and their industrialization for new store concepts.
highlights: Design and architecture
API development
Development management and monitoring
Relations with external partners
Technical Environment: Java, Spring Boot, MongoDB, PostgreSQL, Azure, Gitlab, Gitlab CI

work.name: Leroy Merlin (via Ineat Group)

highlights: Agile facilitation of the team
Slackbot development
Synchronization between teams
Participation in designs and architectures
Technical Environment: Java, Spring Boot, MongoDB, Kafka, Docker, Kubernetes, GCP, Github, Github Actions

work.name: Adeo (via Ineat Group)
work.summary: Intervention in 4 teams to introduce them to the basics of agility and support them in their evolution with Scrum or Kanban.

work.name: Oney Bank (via Ineat Group)
work.summary: In the IT department's project division: tokenization and mobile payment service, strong customer authentication, and exposure of business services as APIs.
highlights: Implement Scrum and support the team
Provide technical assistance on legal aspects
Conduct preliminary studies
Perform technical and functional designs
Participate in architecture implementation
Develop Proofs of Concept
Technical Environment: Java, Spring Boot

work.name: Leroy Merlin (via Ineat Group)
work.summary: Mobility division: mobile sales application prototype. Then consumer mobile applications team: oversee the complete redesign of iOS and Android applications.
highlights: Write technical designs
Follow user workshops and implement ideas (Lean Startup)
Coordinate exchanges between the team and feature teams
Organize and monitor development
Monitor budget
Proximity management of the team (6 to 10 people)
Manage technical and contractual relationships with solution providers
Technical Environment: Android, iOS, Java

work.name: Humanis (via Ineat Group)
highlights: Manage a team of 2 people
Project planning (Planisware tool)
Monitor budget
Coordinate different stakeholders
Manage MOA relationships
Perform designs and development (Java)

work.name: Sopra Group
highlights: Technical Environment: Java, Hibernate, Struts, JSP, Oracle, PostgreSQL, Websphere, Tomcat

work.name: Supinfo Lille
work.summary: Teaching: supervision of blockchain practical labs.

projects.name: Instampy.fr
projects.description: Founder & architect. B2C digital timestamping SaaS (EURL Blue Mossa). Solo project end-to-end, from architectural decisions through production release.
highlights: Cloud-agnostic full-stack architecture with clear separation of concerns and abstraction layers to limit vendor lock-in
Modular multi-chain blockchain design (Sui + Avalanche, extensible), custom smart contracts (Move + Solidity), gas fee risk containment
SSR frontend, REST API, relational database, FR/EN multilingual UI, online payments, full GDPR compliance
Multi-environment deployment on Railway, GitHub Actions CI/CD, uptime monitoring, DNS, DMARC/DKIM
Nuxt 4, Vue 3, TypeScript, Node.js, Express, PostgreSQL, Prisma, Supabase, Railway, Sui, Avalanche, Move, Solidity, Stripe, Cursor

projects.name: Solving Cain's Jawbone
projects.description: AI-based tool to facilitate the resolution of Cain's Jawbone, a complex literary problem.
highlights: Definition of entities and relationships in French and English texts of the work
Writing prompts for data extraction and structuring
Use of vector databases for storage and search
Creation of a knowledge graph to improve the relevance of vector search
Python, Jupyter, Ollama, OpenWebUI, Neo4J, RAG, GraphRAG, Knowledge Graph, Cursor, Model Context Protocols

projects.name: Tezocracy
projects.description: Online tool to facilitate Tezos bakers' votes for governance proposals.
highlights: Development in collaboration with MIDL.dev
React, Taquito, Tezos, Github Action, Netlify

projects.name: Tezos Testnet Faucet
projects.description: Faucet for Tezos testnets, allowing distribution of Tez tokens to Tezos addresses.
highlights: Personal development initially
Obtained funding from the Tezos Foundation to evolve it and integrate it as an official faucet
Handover to OxheadAlpha and maintenance, before transfer to the foundation
React, Taquito, Tezos, Github Actions, Netlify
